concept: Fly / Move Through Air on Wings or Wind
description: To fly: the bird ('pelori'), the seed on the wind, the kite. Motion through air. Deliberately kin in sound to 'wapho' (throw) — thrown things and flying things share the air — but a full syllable apart.
sound_symbolism: 'wa' is the air-glide, 'pi' the small lift-point of a wingbeat. Light sounds for weightless motion.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive: 'pelori wapi.' (The bird flies.) With direction: 'lo pelori wei ronua wapi.' (The birds fly south.) Of seeds: 'thinoe nua howeli wapi.' (Seeds fly with the wind.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Flight without combustion — bird, seed, kite — is the aesthetic of the possible future: lift borrowed from wind.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The farmer read the year in what flew: first swallows, seed-drift, the geese going south — flight as calendar.

tags.nature: The motion of birds and wind-borne things
